acceptance_note: APPROVE_WITH_EXCEPTIONS. The owner approves VS4-H01 for external-user VS5 entry after reviewing the current Drop, Artifact, Ask, Brief, citation, and Decision-draft journey. Remaining exception: Ask and answer records are saved, but the History experience does not make past questions and answers sufficiently visible or easy to revisit. History should be improved or implemented as a clearer user-facing feature.
issue_list: Ask and answer records are persisted locally, but the current UI does not provide a clear first-class conversation history for finding and reopening them.
The UI emphasizes the resulting Brief and Audit trail, so a user can reasonably be unsure whether the original question and generated answer were saved.
The detailed interaction design and acceptance criteria for improved History remain to be defined; this review does not claim that improvement is implemented.
misleading_or_unsafe_states: The current History presentation may mislead users into thinking an Ask/answer was not retained because there is no obvious conversation-history surface, even though the records and audit events are stored.
redaction_note: No raw secrets, provider credentials, private keys, session cookies, bearer tokens, live provider payloads, or unredacted private data are included. This record captures the owner's explicit chat decision and repo-local evidence references.
task_outcomes: VS5 external-user sessions are authorized to begin; History improvement is not a blocker for entry.
History discoverability remains an explicit product exception and must not be described as implemented or accepted.
usability_findings: The current Drop to Artifact to Ask to Brief journey is acceptable for external-user entry.
Redirecting to the Artifact after each upload is understandable after explanation but adds navigation during multi-source intake.
Past Ask questions and generated answers are not discoverable enough from the normal user workspace.
A better History feature should make saved Ask/answer interactions visibly retrievable while preserving links to the resulting Brief and sources; the exact design remains open.
